title: ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| join type=left max=0 admin [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(change_id) as change_id by submitter
    | rename submitter as admin
  ]
| where isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id
value: We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.
implementation: (1) Map Panorama admin IDs to ITSM submitter IDs. (2) Require change_id in commit messages where supported. (3) Weekly governance report. (4) Escalate repeat offenders.
visualization: Table (unapproved commits), Timeline, Bar chart (by admin)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
